primary_sources.title: Unicode Technical Standard #39: Unicode Security Mechanisms

finding: Confusable uniqueness must be computed from a version-pinned skeleton. UTS #39 internalSkeleton applies NFD, removes Default_Ignorable_Code_Point characters, maps each scalar through confusables.txt with identity fallback, and reapplies NFD. Skeleton equality is collision evidence, not evidence of malicious intent.
design_implication: Parse confusables.txt fail-closed, retain Unicode version and source digest, require an independently version-matched default-ignorable predicate, and keep the collision registry separate from Identifier_Status eligibility.

verification.route: isolated Node 22 compatible runtime using byte-equivalent committed module logic

uncertainty: The complete Unicode 17.0.0 confusables data file was not downloaded into the repository in this run.
The supplied source digest is retained but not cryptographically verified by the module.
This artifact implements internalSkeleton, not the complete bidiSkeleton algorithm.
Default_Ignorable_Code_Point data must be supplied from the same Unicode version.
falsification_route: Run the committed parser against the complete authenticated Unicode 17.0.0 confusables.txt, compare skeleton outputs with a conformant reference implementation and Unicode examples, test idempotency over every mapping, and reject the artifact if any mapping, normalization, default-ignorable, or collision result diverges.
trust_limit: Equal skeletons establish confusability under the supplied data version; they do not establish spoofing intent, identity, or claim truth.
